column5: Hand-rolled limiter lacks any acknowledgment of single-process limitation or upgrade path (no comment/marker/note), and createRateLimiter is an unrequested parameterized factory for a single fixed-parameter call site.

Hand-rolled limiter ships with no acknowledgment of its single-process ceiling or upgrade path (no comment, marker, or prose note), and createRateLimiter is an unrequested parameterized factory for one call site.
In-memory Map limiter with acknowledged limitation and suggested mitigation (periodic cleanup) in the Ceiling note; no premature infrastructure or single-use abstraction.

The hand-rolled limiter lacks clear acknowledgment of its limitation or upgrade path; 'For a single process, this is efficient' describes the context but doesn't explicitly name the ceiling (single-process only) or upgrade trigger (Redis when scaling).

In-memory limiter with Map is the right shortcut for single-process; the prose note 'works fine for single-process deployments' acknowledges the constraint, and no premature infrastructure or factory abstraction is present.
